organization-configurations.title: List private registries for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

organization-configurations.descriptionHTML:
Lists all private registry configurations available at the organization-level without revealing their encrypted values.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

items.title: Organization private registry
items.description: Private registry configuration for an organization

name.description: The name of the private registry configuration.

auth_type.description: The authentication type for the private registry.

url.description: The URL of the private registry.

username.description: The username to use when authenticating with the private registry.

replaces_base.description: Whether this private registry replaces the base registry (e.g., npmjs.org for npm, rubygems.org for rubygems). When `true`, Dependabot will only use this registry and will not fall back to the public registry. When `false` (default), Dependabot will use this registry for scoped packages but may fall back to the public registry for other packages.

visibility.description: Which type of organization repositories have access to the private registry.

tenant_id.description: The tenant ID of the Azure AD application.

client_id.description: The client ID of the Azure AD application.

organization-configurations.title: Create a private registry for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: registry_type
bodyParameters.description: The registry type.

bodyParameters.name: url
bodyParameters.description: The URL of the private registry.

bodyParameters.name: username
bodyParameters.description: The username to use when authenticating with the private registry. This field should be omitted if the private registry does not require a username for authentication.

bodyParameters.name: replaces_base
bodyParameters.description: Whether this private registry should replace the base registry (e.g., npmjs.org for npm, rubygems.org for rubygems). When set to true, Dependabot will only use this registry and will not fall back to the public registry. When set to false (default), Dependabot will use this registry for scoped packages but may fall back to the public registry for other packages.

bodyParameters.name: encrypted_value
bodyParameters.description: The value for your secret, encrypted with LibSodium using the public key retrieved from the Get private registries public key for an organization endpoint. Required when auth_type is token or username_password. Should be omitted for OIDC auth types.

bodyParameters.name: key_id
bodyParameters.description: The ID of the key you used to encrypt the secret. Required when auth_type is token or username_password. Should be omitted for OIDC auth types.

bodyParameters.name: visibility
bodyParameters.description: Which type of organization repositories have access to the private registry. selected means only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids can access the private registry.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository IDs that can access the organization private registry. You can only provide a list of repository IDs when visibility is set to selected. You can manage the list of selected repositories using the Update a private registry for an organization endpoint. This field should be omitted if visibility is set to all or private.

bodyParameters.name: auth_type
bodyParameters.description: The authentication type for the private registry. Defaults to token if not specified. Use oidc_azure, oidc_aws, or oidc_jfrog for OIDC authentication.

bodyParameters.name: tenant_id
bodyParameters.description: The tenant ID of the Azure AD application. Required when auth_type is oidc_azure.

bodyParameters.name: client_id
bodyParameters.description: The client ID of the Azure AD application. Required when auth_type is oidc_azure.

bodyParameters.name: aws_region
bodyParameters.description: The AWS region. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: account_id
bodyParameters.description: The AWS account ID. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: role_name
bodyParameters.description: The AWS IAM role name. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: domain
bodyParameters.description: The CodeArtifact domain. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: domain_owner
bodyParameters.description: The CodeArtifact domain owner (AWS account ID). Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: jfrog_oidc_provider_name
bodyParameters.description: The JFrog OIDC provider name. Required when auth_type is oidc_jfrog.

bodyParameters.name: audience
bodyParameters.description: The OIDC audience. Optional for oidc_aws and oidc_jfrog auth types.

bodyParameters.name: identity_mapping_name
bodyParameters.description: The JFrog identity mapping name. Optional for oidc_jfrog auth type.

organization-configurations.descriptionHTML:
Creates a private registry configuration with an encrypted value for an organization. Encrypt your secret using LibSodium. For more information, see "Encrypting secrets for the REST API." For OIDC-based registries (oidc_azure, oidc_aws, or oidc_jfrog), the encrypted_value and key_id fields should be omitted.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

request.description: Example of a private registry configuration with private visibility

When `true`, Dependabot will only use this registry and will not fall back to the public registry. When `false` (default), Dependabot will use this registry for scoped packages but may fall back to the public registry for other packages.

visibility.description: Which type of organization repositories have access to the private registry. `selected` means only the repositories specified by `selected_repository_ids` can access the private registry.

organization-configurations.title: Get private registries public key for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

organization-configurations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the org public key, which is needed to encrypt private registry secrets. You need to encrypt a secret before you can create or update secrets.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

organization-configurations.title: Get a private registry for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

organization-configurations.descriptionHTML:
Get the configuration of a single private registry defined for an organization, omitting its encrypted value.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: The specified private registry configuration for the organization

When `true`, Dependabot will only use this registry and will not fall back to the public registry. When `false` (default), Dependabot will use this registry for scoped packages but may fall back to the public registry for other packages.

organization-configurations.title: Update a private registry for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: registry_type
bodyParameters.description: The registry type.

bodyParameters.name: url
bodyParameters.description: The URL of the private registry.

bodyParameters.name: username
bodyParameters.description: The username to use when authenticating with the private registry. This field should be omitted if the private registry does not require a username for authentication.

bodyParameters.name: replaces_base
bodyParameters.description: Whether this private registry should replace the base registry (e.g., npmjs.org for npm, rubygems.org for rubygems). When set to true, Dependabot will only use this registry and will not fall back to the public registry. When set to false (default), Dependabot will use this registry for scoped packages but may fall back to the public registry for other packages.

bodyParameters.name: encrypted_value
bodyParameters.description: The value for your secret, encrypted with LibSodium using the public key retrieved from the Get private registries public key for an organization endpoint.

bodyParameters.name: key_id
bodyParameters.description: The ID of the key you used to encrypt the secret.

bodyParameters.name: visibility
bodyParameters.description: Which type of organization repositories have access to the private registry. selected means only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids can access the private registry.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository IDs that can access the organization private registry. You can only provide a list of repository IDs when visibility is set to selected. This field should be omitted if visibility is set to all or private.

bodyParameters.name: auth_type
bodyParameters.description: The authentication type for the private registry. This field cannot be changed after creation. If provided, it must match the existing auth_type of the configuration. To change the authentication type, delete and recreate the configuration.

bodyParameters.name: tenant_id
bodyParameters.description: The tenant ID of the Azure AD application. Required when auth_type is oidc_azure.

bodyParameters.name: client_id
bodyParameters.description: The client ID of the Azure AD application. Required when auth_type is oidc_azure.

bodyParameters.name: aws_region
bodyParameters.description: The AWS region. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: account_id
bodyParameters.description: The AWS account ID. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: role_name
bodyParameters.description: The AWS IAM role name. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: domain
bodyParameters.description: The CodeArtifact domain. Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: domain_owner
bodyParameters.description: The CodeArtifact domain owner (AWS account ID). Required when auth_type is oidc_aws.

bodyParameters.name: jfrog_oidc_provider_name
bodyParameters.description: The JFrog OIDC provider name. Required when auth_type is oidc_jfrog.

bodyParameters.name: audience
bodyParameters.description: The OIDC audience. Optional for oidc_aws and oidc_jfrog auth types.

bodyParameters.name: identity_mapping_name
bodyParameters.description: The JFrog identity mapping name. Optional for oidc_jfrog auth type.

organization-configurations.descriptionHTML:
Updates a private registry configuration with an encrypted value for an organization. Encrypt your secret using LibSodium. For more information, see "Encrypting secrets for the REST API." For OIDC-based registries (oidc_azure, oidc_aws, or oidc_jfrog), the encrypted_value and key_id fields should be omitted.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

organization-configurations.title: Delete a private registry for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

organization-configurations.descriptionHTML:
Delete a private registry configuration at the organization-level.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.
